title: Anil aspera
intro: Anil aspera, also known as Aconitum heterophyllum, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Ranunculaceae, commonly found in the Himalayan region. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Anil aspera include alkaloids, such as aconitine and aconine, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Anil aspera is often made into a decoction or a powder, and is also used to prepare Ayurvedic formulations, such as churnas and tablets, to treat various health conditions.
